items.blurb: A tiny warren of zodiac bunnies to hop across January spreads and greeting cards. Once these 32 are gone, they are gone.
An heirloom-feeling A5 with a gold-embossed black spine, made by a notebook house nearly a century old and touched with eric's quiet charm.
Bold textile-designer stripes with pups tucked in and a wink of foil. One roll and your journal has main-character energy.
Field-guide flying squirrels glide down a full seven metres of tape. Naturalist charm for envelopes, spreads and gift wrap.
Fifty-four little slices of the good life for your planner: small joys, sorted into nine designs and ready to scatter.
Illustrator Yoko staffs an entire cafe with animals and lets you seat them in your journal. Fifty-four customers, endlessly re-bookable.
Cats among blooms, drawn with a light and lovely hand. A garden party for your planner, fifty-four guests deep.
Fresh-from-the-oven sticker loaves and pastries by Fukuwake Hanko. Your journal will smell faintly of croissants, we swear.
Crystal-clear stamps that turn October into tiny illustrated headlines. Snip the tab, file it away, stamp all year.
Crystal-clear stamps that turn everyday moments into tiny illustrated headlines. Snip the tab, file it away, stamp all year.
Midori went full lavender: one sheet of purple-tinted pretties to colour-code your week into something worth opening.
Pocketable A6 notebooks from a 1936 Tokyo maker, dressed up by stamp artist eric. Years in the making, seconds to fall for.
Twenty A4 sheets of eric-illustrated paper begging to become wrapping, collage or the world's cutest origami mistakes.
Postcards from eric celebrating a beloved 1936 notebook maker. Send one, frame one, hoard both. We won't judge.
Crystal-clear stamps that turn September into tiny illustrated headlines. Snip the tab, file it away, stamp all year.
Maruco Art shrinks a whole Japan holiday into one stampable square. Instant travel-journal wanderlust, no passport needed.
A pocket-sized cafe scene by Taiwanese artist Maruco Art, ready to press cosy coffee vibes onto any page.
A whisper-thin ribbon of feathers kissed with gold foil. Underline things beautifully; regret nothing.
Rub-on dried florals that transfer straight onto the page — no sticky edges, just seamless painted-in prettiness.
Autumn scenes you rub, not stick: MU transfers melt into your journal page like they were printed there all along.
A miniature botanical world in deep green: thirty delicate flakes for tonal, grown-up journal spreads.
A miniature botanical world in pink: thirty delicate flakes for tonal, grown-up journal spreads.
A miniature botanical world in light blue: thirty delicate flakes for tonal, grown-up journal spreads.
A row of tulips with a flash of foil, unspooling five metres of instant spring for letters, wrap and journals.
A row of daisies with a flash of foil, unspooling five metres of instant spring for letters, wrap and journals.
Metallic-flecked blue washi that catches the light like sea glass. The simplest way to make a plain page feel expensive.
Silver-foiled homebody stickers from Mind Wave's Colors line — soft botanical domestic bliss with a limited-run shimmer.
SOU SOU's cheerful flock lands on shimmering mt tape. Japanese textile-design joy by the metre.
mina perhonen blooms in daylight shades on sparkle-dusted mt tape. Fashion-house florals for your humble planner.
The evening edition of mina perhonen's bloom: deeper tones, same secret sparkle, all over your night-time journalling.
Pottering Cat pins a cat mid-snack on a rice cracker onto your tote, jacket or pouch. Barely an inch of cat, maximum personality.
Pottering Cat pins a kitty curled into a teacup onto your tote, jacket or pouch. Barely an inch of cat, maximum personality.
Pottering Cat pins a cat off on a tiny road trip onto your tote, jacket or pouch. Barely an inch of cat, maximum personality.
Pottering Cat pins a jet-setting little cat onto your tote, jacket or pouch. Barely an inch of cat, maximum personality.
Pottering Cat pins a sporty rugby-playing cat onto your tote, jacket or pouch. Barely an inch of cat, maximum personality.
Pottering Cat pins a determined rugby cat mid-charge onto your tote, jacket or pouch. Barely an inch of cat, maximum personality.
Pottering Cat pins a cat lounging in a strawberry field onto your tote, jacket or pouch. Barely an inch of cat, maximum personality.
Pottering Cat pins a cat taking afternoon tea very seriously onto your tote, jacket or pouch. Barely an inch of cat, maximum personality.
Illustrator eric carved a handmade-with-love motif into a limited-run stamp. When they sell out, that's the whole story.
Illustrator eric carved a good-times moment into a limited-run stamp. When they sell out, that's the whole story.
Illustrator eric carved a cosy knit hat into a limited-run stamp. When they sell out, that's the whole story.
An ink pad steeped in konjyo, a deep pigment navy — one of Japan's traditional nature-named shades, ready to meet your stamps.
An ink pad steeped in akane, the colour of sunset sky — one of Japan's traditional nature-named shades, ready to meet your stamps.
An ink pad steeped in tokusa, a horsetail-plant green — one of Japan's traditional nature-named shades, ready to meet your stamps.
An ink pad steeped in mikan, bright mandarin-peel orange — one of Japan's traditional nature-named shades, ready to meet your stamps.
An ink pad steeped in kobai, soft red-plum blossom pink — one of Japan's traditional nature-named shades, ready to meet your stamps.
Osaka letterpress labels from a two-person studio: three stately frame designs for jars, gifts and journal headers.
Bofa Huang's hundred-flowers washi comes on release paper, so every bloom peels off like its own tiny sticker.
A girl in a straw hat, carved into a stamp by Taiwanese artist Bofa Huang. Summer nostalgia in one press.
Letterpress-style numerals on a roll — date your spreads, number your jars, organise beautifully by accident.
A roll of hand-lettered gratitude: tear off a thank you for every parcel, envelope and lunchbox note.
Five letterpress frame patterns, pressed in Osaka and gone when they're gone. Label your world while you can.
The Minimal edition: five quietly perfect letterpress frames for people whose jars deserve better labels.
Three collectible letterpress frames from a tiny Osaka studio, in a run that will not repeat. Write accordingly.
The Classic edition: three timeless letterpress frames, gently debossed and endlessly useful for gifts and jars.
A wandering cat pauses at a summer farm in Toshinori Mori's Tabi Neko series. Mail it, or let it summer on your shelf.
Sketchy wardrobe stickers for planning outfits you may or may not actually wear. Limited run, so commit to the look.
KITTA's card-sized sticker book slips mirror-shine foil strips into any pocket. Pre-cut, portable, quietly luxurious.
KITTA's card-sized sticker book slips elegant frame foil strips into any pocket. Pre-cut, portable, quietly luxurious.
A full sheet of tiny penguins waddling through your week. Planner morale, restored.
A full sheet of shibas being extremely shiba across your to-do list. Motivation with a curly tail.
Loose, hand-drawn animal pals in sticker form — a limited-quantity zoo for margins and to-do lists.
Zebra's cult Mildliners in the Natural palette: soft, no-smudge shades with a fat tip and a fine one. Notes, but gentler.
Zebra's cult Mildliners in the Yasashisa palette: soft, no-smudge shades with a fat tip and a fine one. Notes, but gentler.
Miki Tamura layers torn washi into little collages of family life, then turns them into stickers. Texture you can practically feel.
Miki Tamura layers torn washi into little collages of celebrations, then turns them into stickers. Texture you can practically feel.
Miki Tamura layers torn washi into little collages of travel, then turns them into stickers. Texture you can practically feel.
Six travel-hued washi tapes — desert, lakeside, even outer space — collaged by artist Miki Tamura into rollable landscapes.
Thirty yellow-toned blossom flakes for building single-shade flower beds across your spreads.
Thirty blue-toned blossom flakes for building single-shade flower beds across your spreads.
Thirty pink-toned blossom flakes for building single-shade flower beds across your spreads.
Thirty red-toned blossom flakes for building single-shade flower beds across your spreads.
Thirty purple-toned blossom flakes for building single-shade flower beds across your spreads.
Thirty green-toned blossom flakes for building single-shade flower beds across your spreads.
Thirty orange-toned blossom flakes for building single-shade flower beds across your spreads.
Springtime in tape form: a five-metre flower border with foil highlights that glint as the light moves.
Polar bears on holiday, printed on Mino washi. Twenty summer-limited flakes of bears doing beach things.
Penguins clutching shaved ice and Japanese summer treats, on soft Mino washi. Twenty flakes of pure seasonal delight.
Wakimichi Stamp hand-carved the moon enjoying a quiet coffee for this dreamy little stamp. Lunar softness on demand.
A moon drifting off to sleep, carved by hand by Wakimichi Stamp. For journal corners that need a gentle goodnight.
